title: Gavi supported countries
subtitle: Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.
caption:
Gavi supported countries

Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.

This choropleth map displays the global distribution of countries eligible for Gavi support in 2024, using a binary color scheme to distinguish between nations that qualify for assistance and those that do not. The legend defines two categories: teal indicates countries eligible for support, while light beige marks those not eligible. The teal shading covers a contiguous geographic band across sub-Saharan Africa, including Nigeria, Ethiopia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Kenya, and Tanzania, alongside most nations in West Africa such as Senegal, Mali, and Niger. In Asia, the eligible category includes Afghanistan, Pakistan, India, Bangladesh, Myanmar, Nepal, and Yemen. The map also highlights eligibility for Papua New Guinea in Oceania, North Korea in East Asia, and several Caribbean island nations including Haiti. The light beige color dominates the landmass of North America, South America, Europe, Australia, and large portions of the Middle East and East Asia, indicating these regions fall outside the income threshold for support. The visual pattern shows a concentration of eligible countries in the lower-income regions of the Global South, specifically forming a broad belt across the African continent and South Asia, while higher-income regions in the Northern Hemisphere and the Southern Ocean remain unshaded.
